html:
Monster Meat is a Food Item obtained by killing most Monsters. Most monsters will drop Monster Meat at least 50% of the time, otherwise dropping their special resources (for example Spiders also drop Silk and Spider Gland). It counts both as Monster Food and Meat.

Monster Meat is toxic and will reduce Health and Sanity when consumed, with the exception of Webber and Wilba, who will not take any penalties upon consumption. Wormwood does not take the health penalty from consuming Monster Meat, but does lose Sanity.

Drop table

Mob(s)/Structure(s) Chance Quantity
() 0.46% 3

() 100% 4-6
() 100% 6-16

Cooked Monster Meat

Cooked Monster Meat is Monster Meat that has been cooked on a Campfire, Fire Pit or Star.

Mobs that ordinarily drop Monster Meat and die from being set on fire, will drop a Cooked Monster Meat instead. Cooking Monster Meat on a Campfire/Fire Pit or drying it on a Drying Rack to make a Monster Jerky reduces its toxic effects. Additionally, one raw, cooked, or dried Monster Meat (or Durian) can be used as a meat/filler in Crock Pot recipes with no toxic effects. It should be noted however that using more than 1 piece of Monster Meat (or Durian) in a Crock Pot will typically result in Monster Lasagna, which is just as toxic as eating raw monster meat, with a large sanity drain penalty.

Monster Jerky

Monster Jerky is the product of drying raw Monster Meat on a Drying Rack for 1 Day.

Monster Jerky can be given to the Pig King in exchange for a Gold Nugget, unlike raw Monster Meat. It can also be fed to a Caged Bird to produce an Egg. It also will decrease Sanity and Health but to a lesser degree than the other forms of monster meat.

See general meat uses.

Unharvested Monster Jerky will not start to spoil as long it is left on the Drying Rack, making it possible to keep it fresh for an indefinite amount of time. However this feature was changed in Reign of Giants DLC in which all dried meat does spoil on the Drying Rack at the same speed as if it was left on the ground. Same change was made for any finished meal left on a Crock Pot.

Usage

Monster Meat can be used as bait for meat-eating creatures, such as Spiders or Buzzards.

Giving Monster Meat to a Pig will befriend it. When 4 pieces of Monster Meat are fed to a Pig, it will transform into a Werepig and become hostile. This can sometimes happen accidentally when Pig Villages are close to Spider Dens, as Pigs will eat the Monster Meat from Spiders they kill. Note that Werepigs always drop 2 Meats and 1 Pig Skin. It is useful when you have too many Monster Meat or lack Pig Skin or Meat.

Monster Jerky can be given to the Pig King in exchange for Gold Nuggets.

Cooked Monster Meat or Monster Jerky can be fed to caged birds to produce Eggs. Raw Monster Meat can also be fed to caged birds to produce Eggs in Don't Starve Together.

Monster meat is a very useful ingredient for various Crock Pot recipes:

  • If only a single piece of Monster Meat is used, Monster Lasagna will not be produced. This is very useful in many meat based recipes such as Meatballs, Meaty Stew, Bacon and Eggs, Honey Ham, Honey Nuggets and Pierogi.
  • Monster Lasagna will never form if a Twig is used as an ingredient. This enables the use of two Monster Meats alongside a Twig in a few recipes such as Bacon and Eggs and Fishsticks. Note that the use of a Twig also prevents a number of other useful recipes.
  • Several recipes from the Shipwrecked and Hamlet DLCs have a higher priority than Monster Lasagna making it possible to safely use multiple Monster Meats. A notable example is Surf 'n' Turf.

Some animals are carnivorous or omnivorous and will eat Monster Meat given to them or left laying around. If they are not immune to the negative effects of Monster Meat, eating it causes them to lose Health. This allows an abundance of Monster Meats to be converted to other resources by simply feeding these animals with Monster Meat directly or indirectly. For example, in Shipwrecked DLC Crabbits and Seagulls will be attracted to and eat Monster Meat, yielding Fish Morsels, and Azure Feathers or Morsels respectively when they eat enough.

In a pinch, Monster Meat can be eaten to stay alive, at a cost. However, Wigfrid can viably use Cooked Monster Meat as a food source, mainly because she has a health and sanity regeneration effect when fighting.

Monster Meat farming is a very practical thing to do especially in the earlier stages of the game because it is very easy and effective.

Downloadable Content

In the Reign of Giants and Shipwrecked DLCs, Monster Jerky will begin to spoil immediately upon creation on a Drying Rack, regardless of whether it is harvested or not.

Recipes

Tips

  • While Monster Meat damages health when eaten it still restores a big chunk of durability for both the Eye Mask and the Shield of Terror.
  • Monster Meat lasts 15 days when cooked, and only 6 days when it's raw. Therefore, it's best to cook it as soon as possible in the early stages of the game to extend its freshness, even when the player gets an Ice Box. However, when Drying Racks become available, even spoiled Monster Meat can be turned into fresh Monster Jerky that lasts 20 days, or 40 days in an Ice Box. The best overall way to extend the spoilage time is to first put the raw Monster Meat into a Salt Box, where it can last up to 24 days. Then it can be dried into Monster Jerky to refresh its spoilage, and subsequently stored in an Ice Box for up to another 40 days before turning into Rot.
  • In Reign of Giants, the Bearger will eat and be damaged by Monster Meat. It takes 150 monster meat to completely kill a Bearger. While this is impractical a full stack takes a substantial chunk of the Bearger's health.
  • Webber starts with 2 Monster Meat and suffers no Health or sanity penalties when eating it. Unlike other characters, cooking the Monster Meat before consuming it provides no bonuses to Webber aside from increased spoiling time.
  • Monster Meat is possibly the greatest of the Crock Pot ingredients/fillers, as it has a meat value of 1, can be turned into jerky and can be made into Eggs with a Bird Cage. This makes it possible to survive off of Monster Meat alone by cooking Meatballs with Monster Meat and Eggs. If a player has access to morsels, Bacon and Eggs can also be made, and is a more beneficial recipe than Meatballs.
  • Feeding cooked monster meat to a bird in a birdcage gives eggs, which can be converted to gold via Pig King.
  • 4 monster meat can be "traded" for 2 regular Meat and a Pig Skin by giving them to a Pig and killing the resulting Werepig. However, since Monster Meat can already replace Meat in most contexts, the main value for this is making Jerky, Meaty Stew or a Ham Bat.
  • Since Monster Jerky does not technically count as a cooked food item, Wagstaff will suffer an additional 3 penalty for eating it, meaning it will instead damage him for 6 .
  • Compared to Cooked Monster Meat, Monster Jerky has half the sanity penalty. As Monster Meat takes 2 days to dry whereas Cooking Monster Meat takes only half a second to cook, it is more worthwhile to cook it instead of drying it except for preservation.
    • As Webber, Monster Jerky provides no additional bonuses over raw Monster Meat.

Trivia

  • Warly states that the meat has "nice marbling", suggesting that it's rather fatty.
  • Contrary to popular belief, WX-78 still takes damage when they eat this.
  • Wickerbottom's quote to a Big Woby implies that there is some kind of mutagen in the monster's meat. This may explain the transformation of Woby into a big form, the transformation of pigs into werepigs and other local abilities of this meat.


Gallery

  • Monster Jerky as seen in the poster for A Little Rain Must Fall update.
  • Monster meat in Terraria
  • Original HD Cooked Monster Meat icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Original HD Monster Meat icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

"monstermeat"
“Ugh. I don't think I should eat that.”
–Wilson, when examining Monster Meat.
“Gross. It's full of hairs.”
–Willow, when examining Monster Meat.
“It is not looking like food.”
–Wolfgang, when examining Monster Meat.
“Evilness pervades it.”
–Wendy, when examining Monster Meat.
“IT IS INCOMPATIBLE WITH MY CHEMICAL ENGINE”
–WX-78, when examining Monster Meat.
“It would be hazardous to consume this.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining Monster Meat.
“I'm not hungry enough for that.”
–Woodie, when examining Monster Meat.
“How revolting.”
–Maxwell, when examining Monster Meat.
“Interesting, it is neither a red meat nor a white meat.”
–Wagstaff, when examining Monster Meat.
“Meat öf the dark beasts.”
–Wigfrid, when examining Monster Meat.
“Smells foul.”
–Webber, when examining Monster Meat.
“Am I really hungry enough?”
–Walani, when examining Monster Meat.
“Hmmm, nice marbling...”
–Warly, when examining Monster Meat.
“Bit sour thet is.”
–Woodlegs, when examining Monster Meat.
“WILBA EATS ALL THINGS”
–Wilba, when examining Monster Meat.
“Belly stuff?”
–Wormwood, when examining Monster Meat.
“What's that smell? It smells like... horror?”
–Wheeler, when examining Monster Meat.
“Hooboy! Is that even meat?”
–Winona, when examining Monster Meat.
“I hope *I* don't look like that inside.”
–Wortox, when examining Monster Meat.
“Glurgh, nuh-uh.”
–Wurt, when examining Monster Meat.
“Uhh Woby? Do you want this?”
–Walter, when examining Monster Meat.
“If I ever want to poison myself with rancid meat, I’ll be sure to try it.”
–Wanda, when examining Monster Meat.

"cookedmonstermeat"
“That's only somewhat more appetizing than when it was raw.”
–Wilson, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“It's still gross.”
–Willow, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“I still do not want to eat this.”
–Wolfgang, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“Cooked evil is still evil.”
–Wendy, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“IT IS STILL SOMEWHAT INCOMPATIBLE”
–WX-78, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“It's still a little bit poisonous.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“I still don't want to eat it.”
–Woodie, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“I'll eat it if I have to.”
–Maxwell, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“An interesting experiment. Shall I risk it?”
–Wagstaff, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“Mönster beast steak.”
–Wigfrid, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“I don't know what everyone's complaining about.”
–Webber, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“This still looks awful.”
–Walani, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“Could use... uh... I don't even...”
–Warly, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“Thet be bad eatin'.”
–Woodlegs, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“WILBA DOTH EATS!”
–Wilba, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“Smells bad”
–Wormwood, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“I like my monster meat medium roar.”
–Wheeler, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“It's still purple in the middle.”
–Winona, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“I'd still rather not eat it.”
–Wortox, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“Gluuurrrgh.”
–Wurt, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“Is it supposed to have that weird aftertaste?”
–Walter, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.
“Somehow cooking it didn't make it much better.”
–Wanda, when examining Cooked Monster Meat.

“Strange-smelling jerky.”
–Wilson
“It's dry and smells strange.”
–Willow
“Look like leather, taste like leather.”
–Wolfgang
“Meat turned dry and weird-tasting.”
–Wendy
“VILE DEHYDRATED PROTEINS”
–WX-78
“Salty yet satisfying.”
–Wickerbottom
“I still don't want to eat it.”
–Woodie
“My teeth are getting too old for this.”
–Maxwell
“Edible, but not enticing.”
–Wagstaff
“All dried up.”
–Wigfrid
“It's really chewy.”
–Webber
“Tough to chew AND crazy-making!”
–Walani
“Could use... better judgment...”
–Warly
“Dried thet beast, I did.”
–Woodlegs
“'TIS MEAT O' DRIEDNESS”
–Wilba
“Hmmm...”
–Wormwood
“Drying it really seals in the horror.”
–Wheeler
“Drying didn't help none.”
–Winona
“I still do not wish to eat it.”
–Wortox
“Smells even worse now, florp!”
–Wurt
“Hey Woby, it's your favourite!”
–Walter
“All the life's dried out of it.”
–Wanda

Cooking Monster Meat on a Campfire/Fire Pit or drying it on a [[Drying Rack]] to make a [[Monster Jerky]] reduces its toxic effects. Additionally, ''one'' raw, cooked, or [[Monster Jerky|dried]] Monster Meat (or [[Durian]]) can be used as a [[meat]]/[[Crock Pot#Filler|filler]] in Crock Pot recipes with no toxic effects. It should be noted however that using more than 1 piece of Monster Meat (or Durian) in a Crock Pot will typically result in [[Monster Lasagna]], which is just as toxic as eating raw monster meat, with a large sanity drain penalty. {{clear}} ==Monster Jerky== {{Object Infobox |foodType = [[Meats|Meat]] [[File:Meats.png|32px|]] |icon = Monster Jerky.png |image = Monster Jerky Dropped.png |hungerRestored = 18.75 |hpRestored = -3 |sanityRestored = -5 |spoil = 20 Days |stack = 20 |foodValue = [[File:Meats.png|32px|link=Meats]] × 1 [[File:Monster Foods.png|32px|link=Monster Food]] × 1 |spawnCode = "monstermeat_dried"}} {{Quotes |wilson = Strange-smelling jerky. |willow = It's dry and smells strange. |wolfgang = Look like leather, taste like leather. |wendy = Meat turned dry and weird-tasting. |wx78 = VILE DEHYDRATED PROTEINS |wickerbottom = Salty yet satisfying. |woodie = I still don't want to eat it. |maxwell = My teeth are getting too old for this. |wagstaff = Edible, but not enticing. |wigfrid = All dried up. |webber = It's really chewy. |walani = Tough to chew AND crazy-making! |warly = Could use... better judgment... |woodlegs = Dried thet beast, I did. |wilba = 'TIS MEAT O' DRIEDNESS |wheeler = Drying it really seals in the horror. |winona = Drying didn't help none. |wortox = I still do not wish to eat it. |wurt = Smells even worse now, florp! |walter = Hey Woby, it's your favourite! |wanda = All the life's dried out of it. |choose = wolfgang|wormwood = Hmmm...}} '''Monster Jerky''' is the product of drying raw [[Monster Meat]] on a [[Drying Rack]] for 1 [[Day]]. Monster Jerky can be given to the [[Pig King]] in exchange for a [[Gold Nugget]], unlike raw Monster Meat. It can also be fed to a [[Birdcage|Caged]] [[Birds|Bird]] to produce an [[Egg]]. It also will decrease Sanity and Health but to a lesser degree than the other forms of monster meat. See ''[[meats#Usage|general meat uses]]''. Unharvested Monster Jerky will not start to spoil as long it is left on the Drying Rack, making it possible to keep it fresh for an indefinite amount of time. However this feature was changed in ''[[Reign of Giants]]'' DLC in which all dried meat does spoil on the Drying Rack at the same speed as if it was left on the ground. Same change was made for any finished meal left on a Crock Pot. {{clear}} =={{pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage== Monster Meat can be used as bait for meat-eating creatures, such as [[Spiders]] or [[Buzzards]]. Giving Monster Meat to a [[Pig]] will befriend it. When 4 pieces of Monster Meat are fed to a Pig, it will transform into a [[Werepig]] and become hostile. This can sometimes happen accidentally when [[Pig Village]]s are close to [[Spider Dens]], as Pigs will eat the Monster Meat from Spiders they kill. Note that Werepigs always drop 2 Meats and 1 Pig Skin. It is useful when you have too many Monster Meat or lack Pig Skin or Meat. [[Monster Jerky]] can be given to the [[Pig King]] in exchange for [[Gold Nugget]]s. Cooked Monster Meat or Monster Jerky can be fed to [[Birdcage|caged birds]] to produce [[Egg]]s. Raw Monster Meat can also be fed to [[Birdcage|caged birds]] to produce [[Egg]]s in Don't Starve Together. Monster meat is a very useful ingredient for various [[Crock Pot]] recipes: * If only a single piece of Monster Meat is used, [[Monster Lasagna]] will not be produced. This is very useful in many meat based recipes such as [[Meatballs]], [[Meaty Stew]], [[Bacon and Eggs]], [[Honey Ham]], [[Honey Nuggets]] and [[Pierogi]]. * Monster Lasagna will never form if a [[Twigs|Twig]] is used as an ingredient. This enables the use of two Monster Meats alongside a Twig in a few recipes such as [[Bacon and Eggs]] and [[Fishsticks]]. Note that the use of a Twig also prevents a number of other useful recipes. * Several recipes from the Shipwrecked and Hamlet DLCs have a higher priority than Monster Lasagna making it possible to safely use multiple Monster Meats. A notable example is [[Surf 'n' Turf]]. Some [[animals]] are carnivorous or omnivorous and will eat Monster Meat given to them or left laying around. If they are not immune to the negative effects of Monster Meat, eating it causes them to lose Health. This allows an abundance of Monster Meats to be converted to other resources by simply feeding these animals with Monster Meat directly or indirectly. For example, in ''[[Shipwrecked]]'' DLC [[Crabbit]]s and [[Seagull]]s will be attracted to and eat Monster Meat, yielding [[Fish Morsel]]s, and [[Azure Feather]]s or [[Morsel]]s respectively when they eat enough. In a pinch, Monster Meat can be eaten to stay alive, at a cost. However, [[Wigfrid]] can viably use Cooked Monster Meat as a food source, mainly because she has a health and sanity regeneration effect when fighting. Monster Meat farming is a very practical thing to do especially in the earlier stages of the game because it is very easy and effective. == [[File:Gift Icon.png|32px]] Downloadable Content == In the ''[[Reign of Giants]]'' and ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLCs, Monster Jerky will begin to spoil immediately upon creation on a Drying Rack, regardless of whether it is harvested or not. =={{pic|32|Icon Science}} Recipes== {{Recipe |item = Monster Meat |fire = yes |result=Cooked Monster Meat }} {{Recipe |item = Monster Meat |dry = yes |result = Monster Jerky }} {{Recipe |item=Cooked Monster Meat |bird=yes |result=Egg }} {{Recipe |item=Monster Jerky |bird=yes |result=Egg}} {{Recipe |item = Monster Jerky |gold = yes |result = Gold Nugget }} =={{pic|32|Prototype|link=}} Tips== * While Monster Meat damages health when eaten it still restores a big chunk of durability for both the [[Eye Mask]] and the [[Shield of Terror]]. *Monster Meat lasts 15 days when cooked, and only 6 days when it's raw. Therefore, it's best to cook it as soon as possible in the early stages of the game to extend its freshness, even when the player gets an [[Ice Box]]. However, when [[Drying Rack|Drying Racks]] become available, even spoiled Monster Meat can be turned into fresh [[Monster Jerky]] that lasts 20 days, or 40 days in an Ice Box. The best overall way to extend the spoilage time is to first put the raw Monster Meat into a [[Salt Box]], where it can last up to 24 days. Then it can be dried into Monster Jerky to refresh its spoilage, and subsequently stored in an Ice Box for up to another 40 days before turning into [[Rot]]. * In ''[[Reign of Giants]]'', the [[Bearger]] will eat and be damaged by Monster Meat. It takes 150 monster meat to completely kill a Bearger. While this is impractical a full stack takes a substantial chunk of the Bearger's health. * [[Webber]] starts with 2 Monster Meat and suffers no Health or sanity penalties when eating it. Unlike other characters, cooking the Monster Meat before consuming it provides no bonuses to Webber aside from increased spoiling time. * Monster Meat is possibly the greatest of the Crock Pot ingredients/fillers, as it has a meat value of 1, can be turned into jerky and can be made into [[Eggs]] with a [[Bird Cage]]. This makes it possible to survive off of Monster Meat alone by cooking Meatballs with Monster Meat and Eggs. If a player has access to morsels, Bacon and Eggs can also be made, and is a more beneficial recipe than Meatballs. * Feeding cooked monster meat to a bird in a birdcage gives eggs, which can be converted to gold via Pig King. * 4 monster meat can be "traded" for 2 regular [[Meat]] and a [[Pig Skin]] by giving them to a [[Pig]] and killing the resulting [[Werepig]]. However, since Monster Meat can already replace Meat in most contexts, the main value for this is making [[Jerky]], [[Meaty Stew]] or a [[Ham Bat]]. *Since Monster Jerky does not technically count as a cooked food item, [[Wagstaff]] will suffer an additional 3 {{hp}} penalty for eating it, meaning it will instead damage him for 6 {{hp}}. *Compared to Cooked Monster Meat, Monster Jerky has half the sanity penalty. As Monster Meat takes 2 days to dry whereas Cooking Monster Meat takes only half a second to cook, it is more worthwhile to cook it instead of drying it except for preservation. **As [[Webber]], Monster Jerky provides no additional bonuses over raw Monster Meat. =={{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia== * [[Warly]] states that the meat has "nice [[Wikipedia:Marbled meat|marbling]]", suggesting that it's rather fatty. * Contrary to popular belief, [[WX-78]] still takes damage when they eat this. * [[Wickerbottom]]'s quote to a Big [[Woby]] implies that there is some kind of mutagen in the monster's meat. This may explain the transformation of [[Woby]] into a big form, the transformation of [[Pig|pigs]] into [[Werepig|werepigs]] and other local abilities of this meat. =={{pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery == A Little Rain Must Fall.jpg|Monster Jerky as seen in the poster for ''[[Don't Starve/Version History#March 12, 2013 - A Little Rain Must Fall|A Little Rain Must Fall]]'' update.
